title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Package V3 remains deliverable but is under increasing schedule pressure. Overall actual progress is 36.8% versus planned 39.0% , giving a schedule variance of -2.2% . Cost is currently manageable but forecast at completion has moved to RM 1.365 billion , driven mainly by Cheras North disruption, utilities diversion complexity, and prolonged interface coordination.
contextualized_text:
Document: Executive Steering Update Mar 2026.pdf
Document type: progress_update
Reporting period: 2026-03
Reporting date: 20 Mar 2026
Project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
Title: Executive Project Status Update
Section: 1. Headline Position
Page span: 1
Chunk type: text

1. Headline Position
Package V3 remains deliverable but is under increasing schedule pressure. Overall actual progress is 36.8% versus planned 39.0% , giving a schedule variance of -2.2% . Cost is currently manageable but forecast at completion has moved to RM 1.365 billion , driven mainly by Cheras North disruption, utilities diversion complexity, and prolonged interface coordination.

title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Schedule, Status = Red. Schedule, Summary = Delay concentrated in utilities, Cheras North, and interface-related access handover. Cost, Status = Amber. Cost, Summary = Forecast increased but remains within recoverable range if decisions are made now. Safety, Status = Green. Safety, Summary = No LTI; near-miss actions being tracked. Quality, Status = Amber. Quality, Summary = No systemic defect trend, but closeout discipline needs strengthening. Stakeholder / Authority, Status = Amber. Stakeholder / Authority, Summary = Escalation is needed to resolve the diversion approval bottleneck

Stakeholder / Authority, Summary = Escalation is needed to resolve the diversion approval bottleneck

title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text:
- Schedule slippage widened from -1.3% at end-February to -2.2% in mid-March.
- Utility diversion completion for the Cheras North corridor has not yet been fully secured.
- Interface design freeze was not achieved in full by end-March as originally targeted.
- Forecast at completion increased from RM 1.352 billion to RM 1.365 billion .
contextualized_text:
Document: Executive Steering Update Mar 2026.pdf
Document type: progress_update
Reporting period: 2026-03
Reporting date: 20 Mar 2026
Project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
Title: Executive Project Status Update
Section: 3.1 What Changed Since February
Page span: 1
Chunk type: list

3.1 What Changed Since February
- Schedule slippage widened from -1.3% at end-February to -2.2% in mid-March.
- Utility diversion completion for the Cheras North corridor has not yet been fully secured.
- Interface design freeze was not achieved in full by end-March as originally targeted.
- Forecast at completion increased from RM 1.352 billion to RM 1.365 billion .

title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text:
- Core viaduct production remains broadly recoverable if supply and weather stabilise.
- Pandan Gateway Station is only marginally behind target.
- Safety performance remains stable and no major quality trend has emerged.

3.2 What Is Still Going Well
- Core viaduct production remains broadly recoverable if supply and weather stabilise.
- Pandan Gateway Station is only marginally behind target.
- Safety performance remains stable and no major quality trend has emerged.

project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: Utility diversion approval delay, Current View = Severe. Utility diversion approval delay, Executive Note = Requires employer-level escalation with authority. title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text:
1. Approve additional recovery budget for targeted night-shift and weekend operations through April.
2. Endorse alternate precast supply route if the current supplier misses the revised weekly output commitment.
3. Escalate unresolved diversion approval through the employer and relevant authority leadership.

Section: 6. Decisions Required

6. Decisions Required
1. Approve additional recovery budget for targeted night-shift and weekend operations through April.
2. Endorse alternate precast supply route if the current supplier misses the revised weekly output commitment.
3. Escalate unresolved diversion approval through the employer and relevant authority leadership.

title: Executive Project Status Update
project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
raw_text: The project team believes the package can still be recovered without a major baseline reset if March and April actions are implemented decisively. However, if utility diversion approval and interface sign-off remain partial into April, the delay risk will likely shift from recoverable to structural.
contextualized_text:
Document: Executive Steering Update Mar 2026.pdf
Document type: progress_update
Reporting period: 2026-03
Reporting date: 20 Mar 2026
Project: Klang Valley East Metro - Package V3
Title: Executive Project Status Update
Section: 7. Management View
Page span: 3
Chunk type: text

7. Management View
The project team believes the package can still be recovered without a major baseline reset if March and April actions are implemented decisively. However, if utility diversion approval and interface sign-off remain partial into April, the delay risk will likely shift from recoverable to structural.
